author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XXVI. 51-54.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And, behold, one of them which were with Jesus stretched forth his hand, and drew his sword, and struck a servant of the high priest’s, and smote off his ear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then said Jesus unto him, Put up again thy sword unto his place, for all they that take the sword, shall perish by the sword.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thinkestthou that I cannot pray to the Father, and He shall presentlygive me more than twelve legions of angels?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then should the Scriptures be fulfilled that thus it must be?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Who was this “one,” who cut off the ear?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: John saith that it was Peter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the act was of his fervor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But this other point is worth inquiry, wherefore they were bearing swords?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that they bore them is evident not hence only, but from their saying when asked, “here are two.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore did Christ even permit them to have swords?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Luke affirms this too, that He said unto them, “When I sent you without purse, and scrip, and shoes, lacked ye anything?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they said, “Nothing,” He said unto them, “But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and a scrip, and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they said, “Here are two swords,” He said unto them, “It is enough.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore then did He suffer them to have them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To assure them that He was to be betrayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith unto them, “Let him buy a sword,” not that they should arm themselves, far from it; but by this, indicating His being betrayed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore doth He mention a scrip also?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He was teaching them henceforth to be sober, and wakeful, and to use much diligence on their own part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For at the beginning He cherished them (as being inexperienced) with much putting forth of His power but afterwards bringing them forth as young birds out of the nest, He commands them to use their own wings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, that they might not suppose that it was for weakness He is letting them alone, in commanding them also to work their part, He reminds them of the former things, saying, “When I sent you without purse, lacked ye anything?” that by both they might learn His power, both wherein He protected them, and wherein He now leaveth them to themselves by degrees.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But whence were the swords there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They were come forth from the supper, and from the table.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was likely also there should be swords because of the lamb, and that the disciples, hearing that certain were coming forth against Him, took them for defense, as meaning to fight in behalf of their Master, which was of their thought only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Peter is rebuked for using it, and with a severe threat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he was resisting the servant who came, warmly indeed, yet not defending himself, but doing this in behalf of his Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Christ however suffered not any harm to ensue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He healed him, and showed forth a great miracle, enough to indicate at once both His forbearance and His power, and the affection and meekness of His disciple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For then he acted from affection, now with dutifulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when he heard, “Put up thy sword into its sheath,”he obeyed straightway, and afterwards nowhere doeth this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But another saith, that they moreover asked, “Shall we smite?”but that He forbad it, and healed the man, and rebuked His disciple, and threatened, that He might move him to obedience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For all they that take the sword,” He said, “shall die with the sword.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he adds a reason, saying, “Think ye that I cannot pray to my Father, and He shall presently give me more than twelve legions of angels?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that the Scriptures might be fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these words He quenched their anger, indicating that to the Scriptures also, this seemed good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore there too He prayed, that they might take meekly what befell Him, when they had learnt that this again is done according to God’s will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by these two things, He comforted them, both by the punishment of them that are plotting against Him, “For all they,” He saith, “that take the sword shall perish with the sword;” and by His not undergoing these things against His will, “For I can pray,” He saith, “to my Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did He not say, “Think ye that I cannot destroy them all?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because He was more likely to be believed in saying what He did say; for not yet had they the right belief concerning Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And a little while before He had said, “My soul is exceeding sorrowful even unto death,” and, “Father, let the cup pass from me;”and He had appeared in an agony and sweating, and strengthened by an angel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since then He had shown forth many tokens of human nature, He did not seem likely to speak so as to be believed, if He had said, “Think ye that I cannot destroy them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “What, think ye that I cannot pray to my Father?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He speaks it humbly, in saying, “He will presently give me twelve legions of angels.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if one angel slew one hundred and eighty-five armed thousands,what need of twelve legions against a thousand men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He frames His language with a view to their terror and weakness, for indeed they were dead with fear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He brings against them the Scriptures, saying, “How then shall the Scriptures be fulfilled?” alarming them by this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if this be approved by the Scriptures, do ye oppose and fight against them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to His disciples He saith these things; but to the others, “Are ye come out as against a thief with swords and staves for to take me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I sat daily teaching in the temple, and ye laid no hold on me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how many things He doeth that might awaken them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He cast them to the ground, He healed the servant’s ear, He threatened them with being slain; “For they shall perish with the sword,” He saith, “who take the sword.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By the healing of the ear, He gave assurance of these things also; from every quarter, both from the things present, and from the things to come, manifesting His power, and showing that it was not a work of their strength to seize Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He also adds, “I was daily with you, and sat teaching, and ye laid no hold on me;” by this also making it manifest, that the seizure was of His permission.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He passed over the miracles, and mentions the teaching, that He might not seem to boast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When I taught, ye laid no hold on me; when I held my peace, did ye come against me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I was in the temple, and no one seized me, and now do ye come upon me late and at midnight with swords and staves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What need was there of these weapons against Him, who was with you always? by these things teaching them, that unless He had voluntarily yielded, not even then would they have succeeded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither could they (who were not able to hold Him when in their hands, and who, when they had got Him in the midst of them, had not prevailed) even then have succeeded, unless He had been willing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this, He solves also the difficulty why He willed it then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “this was done,” He saith, “that the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See how even up to the last hour, and in the very act of being betrayed, He did all things for their amendment, healing, prophesying, threatening.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For,” He saith, “they shall perish by the sword.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To show that He is suffering voluntarily, He saith, “I was daily with you teaching;” to manifest His accordance with the Father, He adds, “That the Scriptures of the prophets might be fulfilled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore did they not lay hold on Him in the temple?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because they would not have dared in the temple, on account of the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He went forth without, both by the place and by the time giving them security, and even to the last hour taking away their excuse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He who, in order that He might obey the prophets, gave up even Himself, how did He teach things contrary to them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then all His disciples,” it is said, “forsook Him, and fled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when He was seized, they remained; but when He had said these things to the multitudes, they fled.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thenceforth they saw that escape was no longer possible, when He was giving Himself up to them voluntarily, and saying, that this was done according to the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when these were fled, “they lead Him away to Caiaphas; but Peter followed, and entered in to see what the end should be.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Great was the fervor of the disciple; neither did he fly when he saw them flying, but stood his ground, and went in with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if John did so too, yet he was “known to the high priest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why did they lead Him away there where they were all assembled?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they might do all things with consent of the chief priests.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he was then high priest, and all were waiting for Christ there, to such a degree did they spend the whole night, and give up their sleep for this object.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did they then eat the passover, but watched for this other purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For John, when he had said that “it was early,” added, “they entered into the judgment hall, lest they should be defiled, but that they might eat the passover.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What must we say then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they ate it on another day, and broke the law, on account of their eager desire about this murder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For Christ would not have transgressed as to the time of the passover, but they who were daring all things, and trampling under foot a thousand laws.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since they were exceedingly boiling with rage, and having often attempted to seize Him, had not been able; having then taken Him unexpectedly, they chose even to pass by the passover, for the sake of satiating their murderous lust.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they were all assembled together, and it was a council of pestilent men,and they ask some questions, wishing to invest this plot with the appearance of a court of justice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “neither did their testimonies agree together;”so feigned was the court of justice, and all things full of confusion and disorder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But false witnesses came, and said, This fellow said, I will destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And indeed He had said, “In three days,” but He said not, “I will destroy,” but, “Destroy,” and not about that temple but about His own body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then doth the high priest?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Willing to press Him to a defense, that by that he might take Him, he saith, “Hearest Thou not what these witness against Thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He held His peace.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the attempts at defense were unprofitable, no man hearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this was a show only of a court of justice, but in truth an onset of robbers, assailing Him without cause, as in a cave, or on a road.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore “He held His peace,” but the other continued, saying, “I adjure Thee by the living God, that Thou tell us whether Thou be the Christ, the Son of the living God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He said, Thou hast said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless I say unto you, Hereafter shall ye see the Son of Man sitting at the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then the high priest rent his clothes, saying, He hath spoken blasphemy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this he did to add force to the accusation, and to aggravate what He said, by the act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since what had been said moved the hearers to fear, what they did about Stephen,stopping their ears, this high priest doth here also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet what kind of blasphemy was this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed before He had said, when they were gathered together, “The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand,”and interpreted the saying, and they dared say nothing, but held their peace, and from that time forth gainsaid Him no more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then did they now call the saying a blasphemy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore also did Christ thus answer them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To take away all their excuse, because unto the last day He taught that He was Christ, and that He sitteth at the right hand of the Father, and that He will come again to judge the world, which was the language of one manifesting His full accordance with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having rent therefore his clothes, he saith, “What think ye?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He gives not the sentence from himself, but invites it from them, as in a case of confessed sins, and manifest blasphemy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, inasmuch as they knew that if the thing came to be inquired into, and carefully decided, it would free Him from all blame, they condemn Him amongst themselves, and anticipate the hearers by saying, “Ye have heard the blasphemy;” all but necessitating and forcing them to deliver the sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He is guilty of death;” that having taken Him as condemned, they should thus work upon Pilate thereupon to pass sentence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In which matter those others also being accomplices say, “He is guilty of death;” themselves accusing, themselves judging, themselves passing sentence, themselves being everything then.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore did they not bring forward the Sabbaths?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because He had often stopped their mouths; and moreover they wanted to take Him, and condemn Him by the things then said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the high priest anticipated them, and gave the sentence as from them, and drew them all on by rending his vestments, and having led Him away as now condemned unto Pilate, thus did all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Before Pilate at any rate they said nothing of this kind, but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ifthis Man were not a malefactor, we would not have delivered Him up unto thee;” attempting to put Him to death by political accusations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And wherefore did they not slay Him secretly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They were desirous also to bring up an evil report against His fame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since many had now heard Him, and were admiring Him, and amazed at Him, therefore they endeavored that He should be put to death publicly, and in the presence of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But Christ hindered it not, but made full use of their wickedness for the establishment of the truth, so that His death should be manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the result was the contrary to what they wished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they wished to make a show of it, as in this way disgracing Him, but He even by these very things shone forth the more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And much as they said, “Let us put Him to death, lest the Romans come and take away our place and nation;”and after they had put Him to death, this came to pass; so also here; their object was to crucify Him publicly, that they might injure His fame, and the contrary result took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that indeed they had power to have put Him to death, even amongst themselves, hear what Pilate saith: “Take ye Him, and judge Him according to your law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they would not, that He might seem to have been put to death as a transgressor, as an usurper, as a mover of sedition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore also they crucified thieves with Him; therefore also they said, “Write not that this man is King of the Jews; but that He said it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But all these things are done for the truth, so that they might not have so much as any shadow of a defense that is surely shameless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And at the sepulchre too, in the like manner, the seals and the watches made the truth to be the more conspicuous; and the mockings, and the jeerings, and the revilings, wrought again this self-same effect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the nature of error: it is destroyed by those things whereby it plots; thus at least it fell out even here, for they that seemed to have conquered, these most of all were put to shame, and defeated, and ruined; but He that seemed to be defeated, this man above all hath both shone forth, and conquered mightily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then everywhere seek victory, nor everywhere shun defeat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There is an occasion when victory brings hurt, but defeat profit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, for instance, in the case of them that are angry; he that hath been very outrageous seems to have prevailed; but this man above all is the one subdued and hurt by the most grievous passion; but he that hath endured nobly, this man hath got the better and conquered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And while the one hath not had strength to overcome so much as his own disease; the other hath removed another man’s; this hath been subdued by his own, that hath got the better even of another’s passion; and so far from being burnt up, he quenched the flame of another when raised to a height.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if he had minded to gain what seems to be victory, both he himself would have been overcome; and having inflamed the other, he would have occasioned him to have suffered this more grievously; and, like women, both the one and the other would have been disgracefully and miserably overthrown by their anger.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now he that hath exercised self-control is both freed from this disgrace, and hath erected a glorious trophy over anger both in himself and in his neighbor, through his honorable defeat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then everywhere seek victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he that hath overreached hath conquered the person wronged, but with an evil victory, and one that brings destruction to him that has won it; but he that is wronged, and seems to have been conquered, if he have borne it with self-command, this above all is the one that hath the crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For often to be defeated is better, and this is the best mode of victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whether one overreaches, or smites, or envies, he that is defeated, and enters not into the conflict, this is he who hath the victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why do I speak of overreaching and envy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he also that is dragged to martyrdom, thus conquers by being bound, and beaten, and maimed, and slain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is in wars defeat, namely, for the combatant to fall; this with us is victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nowhere do we overcome by doing wrongfully, but everywhere by suffering wrongfully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also doth the victory become more glorious, when we sufferers get the better of the doers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hereby it is shown that the victory is of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it hath an opposite nature to outward conquest, which fact is again above all an infallible sign of strength.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also the rocks in the sea, by being struck, break the waves; thus also all the saints were proclaimed, and crowned, and set up their glorious trophies, winning this tranquil victory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For stir not thyself,” He saith, “neither weary thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: God hath given thee this might, to conquer not by conflict, but by endurance alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Do not oppose thyself also as he does, and thou hast conquered; conflict not, and thou hast gained the crown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou disgrace thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Allow him not to say that by conflicting thou hast got the better, but suffer him to be amazed and to marvel at thy invincible power; and to say to all, that even without entering into conflict thou hast conquered.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also the blessed Joseph obtained a good report, everywhere by suffering wrong getting the better of them who were doing it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For his brethren and the Egyptian woman were amongst those that were plotting against him, but over all did this man prevail.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not of the prison, wherein this man dwelt, nor of the kings’ courts where she abode, but show me who it is that is conquered, who it is that is defeated, who that is in despondency, who that is in pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For she, so far from being able to prevail over the righteous man, could not master so much as her own passion; but this man prevailed both over her and over that grievous disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou wilt, hear her very words, and thou shalt see the trophy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Thou broughtest in unto us here an Hebrew servant to mock us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was not this man that mocked thee, O wretched and unhappy woman, but the devil that told thee that thou couldest break down the adamant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This thy husband brought not in unto thee an Hebrew servant to plot against thee, but the wicked spirit brought in that unclean lasciviousness; he it was that mocked thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then did Joseph?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He held his peace, and thus is condemned, even as Christ is also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For all those things are types of these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he indeed was in bonds, and she in royal courts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he was more glorious than any crowned victor, even while continuing in his bonds, but she was in a more wretched condition than any prisoner, while abiding in royal chambers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But not hence alone may one see the victory, and the defeat, but by the end itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For which accomplished his desired object?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The prisoner, not the high born lady?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he strove to keep his chastity, but she to destroy it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which then accomplished what he desired? he who suffered wrong, or she who did the wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite plain, that it is he who suffered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Surely then this is the one who hath conquered.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowing then these things, let us follow after this victory, which is obtained by suffering wrong, let us flee from that which is got by doing wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so shall we both live this present life in all tranquility, and great quietness, and shall attain unto the good things to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might world without end.
